question: Map Handicap: PSSN.UA (-1.5) vs G2 (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Passion UA and G2 in the IEM Krakow Play-In, scheduled for January 28 at 12:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Passion UA" if Passion UA wins 2 or more maps than G2 in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "G2".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Passion UA vs G2 (BO3) - IEM Krakow Play-In
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Passion UA and G2 in the IEM Krakow Play-In, scheduled for January 28 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Passion UA" if Passion UA win the match against G2.

This market will resolve to "G2" if G2 win the match against Passion UA.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

question: Big Game: First Timeout?
description:
This market will resolve to “NE” if the New England Patriots are the first team to take a timeout during Super Bowl LX.

This market will resolve to “SEA” if the Seattle Seahawks are the first team to take a timeout during Super Bowl LX.

A timeout is considered “taken” when it is officially charged to a team by the game officials, regardless of whether it is requested by a player, coach, or any other reasons. If a team initiates a coach’s challenge and the challenge is unsuccessful, resulting in a timeout being charged to that team, that timeout will count toward this market. If a challenge is successful and no timeout is charged, it will not count toward this market.

Media timeouts, official timeouts (e.g., for injuries, reviews, measurements, or administrative purposes), or stoppages not charged to either team will not count toward this market.

If both teams are charged with a timeout at the same game clock time, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Super Bowl LX is cancelled, postponed after February 22, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or no timeout is charged to either team within that timeframe, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official broadcast and official NFL game statistics for Super Bowl LX; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

description:

question: Counter-Strike: Imperial vs Zingaro Gaming (BO3) - FiReCONTER Group A
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Imperial and Zingaro Gaming in the FiReCONTER Group A, scheduled for January 29 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial" if Imperial win the match against Zingaro Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Zingaro Gaming" if Zingaro Gaming win the match against Imperial.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

question: Venezia FC vs. Modena FC 2018: O/U 1.5
description:
In the upcoming Serie B game between Venezia FC and Modena FC 2018, scheduled for February 10 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Venezia FC and Modena FC 2018 combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

question: Venezia FC vs. Modena FC 2018: Both Teams to Score
description:
In the upcoming Serie B game between Venezia FC and Modena FC 2018, scheduled for February 10 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price ("C") for the EUR/USD candle for the final daily candle on the date specified (ET) in the title is higher than the close price for the final daily candle on the prior trading day once information is finalized for both specified dates. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of the week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market's resolution will be based solely on information from the "C" figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

question: Game Handicap: GL (-1.5) vs Peru Rejects (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between GamerLegion and Peru Rejects in the PGL Wallachia: Americas Closed Qualifier Playoffs, scheduled for January 28 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "GamerLegion" if GamerLegion wins 2 or more games than Peru Rejects in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Peru Rejects".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Solana Up or Down - January 30, 6PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the SOL/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

question: Will NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) beat quarterly earnings?
description:
As of market creation, NXP Semiconductors is estimated to release earnings on February 2, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for NXP Semiconductors’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $3.31 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if NXP Semiconductors reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $3.31 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If NXP Semiconductors releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

question: Will Merck & Co Inc (MRK) beat quarterly earnings?
description:
As of market creation, Merck & Co Inc is estimated to release earnings on February 3, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Merck & Co Inc’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $2.01 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Merck & Co Inc reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $2.01 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Merck & Co Inc releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 30, 7PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will McDonald's (MCD) beat quarterly earnings?
description:
As of market creation, McDonald's is estimated to release earnings on February 11, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for McDonald's’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $3.04 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if McDonald's reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $3.04 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If McDonald's releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

description:

question: Map Handicap: B8 (-1.5) vs paiN (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between paiN and B8 in the IEM Krakow Play-In, scheduled for January 29 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "B8" if B8 wins 2 or more maps than paiN in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "paiN".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: Counter-Strike: paiN vs B8 (BO3) - IEM Krakow Play-In
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between paiN and B8 in the IEM Krakow Play-In, scheduled for January 29 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "paiN" if paiN win the match against B8.

This market will resolve to "B8" if B8 win the match against paiN.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

question: Heart of Midlothian FC vs. Hibernian FC: O/U 1.5
description:
In the upcoming Scottish Cup game between Heart of Midlothian FC and Hibernian FC, scheduled for February 10 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Heart of Midlothian FC and Hibernian FC combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on scottishfa.co.uk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

question: Heart of Midlothian FC vs. Hibernian FC: O/U 3.5
description:
In the upcoming Scottish Cup game between Heart of Midlothian FC and Hibernian FC, scheduled for February 10 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Heart of Midlothian FC and Hibernian FC combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on scottishfa.co.uk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

question: Heart of Midlothian FC vs. Hibernian FC: Both Teams to Score
description:
In the upcoming Scottish Cup game between Heart of Midlothian FC and Hibernian FC, scheduled for February 10 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Heart of Midlothian FC and Hibernian FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on scottishfa.co.uk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

question: Will MGM Resorts (MGM) beat quarterly earnings?
description:
As of market creation, MGM Resorts is estimated to release earnings on February 11, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for MGM Resorts’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.56 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if MGM Resorts reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $0.56 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If MGM Resorts releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 29, 7:30PM-7:45PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 30, 8PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Dota 2: Amaru Gaming vs Heroic (BO3) - PGL Wallachia: Americas Closed Qualifier Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Amaru Gaming and Heroic in the PGL Wallachia: Americas Closed Qualifier Playoffs, scheduled for January 28 at 9:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Amaru Gaming" if Amaru Gaming win the match against Heroic.

This market will resolve to "Heroic" if Heroic win the match against Amaru Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.

question: Australian Open Women's: Kanon Sawashiro vs Ksenia Efremova
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Kanon Sawashiro and Ksenia Efremova in the Australian Open Juniors, Girls, scheduled for January 29 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Sawashiro' if Kanon Sawashiro advances against Ksenia Efremova.

This market will resolve to 'Efremova' if Ksenia Efremova advances against Kanon Sawashiro.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Ethereum Up or Down - January 30, 8PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the ETH/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

In the upcoming match between strongboys and  Pipsqueak+4 in the PGL Wallachia: Western Europe Closed Qualifier Playoffs, scheduled for January 29 at 7:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 51 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

question: XRP Up or Down - January 30, 8PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the XRP/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance XRP/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: First Blood in Game 1?
description:
In the upcoming match between Amaru Gaming and Heroic in the PGL Wallachia: Americas Closed Qualifier Playoffs, scheduled for January 28 at 8:15PM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to the remake. If the first blood did not occur prior to the remake, resolution will be based on the remade game.

If Game 1 is completed but first blood never occurred, this market will resolve to 50-50.

This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Power Rangers and L1ga Team in the PGL Wallachia: Eastern Europe Closed Qualifier Playoffs, scheduled for January 29 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Power Rangers" if Power Rangers win the match against L1ga Team.

This market will resolve to "L1ga Team" if L1ga Team win the match against Power Rangers.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.
